nameMeanings.name: Basset
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from a diminutive of Old French bas(se) ‘short, of low stature’ (see Bas ).
English: variant of   Bassett  , a cognate of 1 above.
In some cases also an altered form of French Besset (see Bessette 2). Compare Bassette 1.

variants.name: Bas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Mauricio, Abelardo, Florencio, Francisco, Jorge, Luis, Manuel, Pedro, Soledad, Vicente.  Eastern Slavic Dmytro, Liya, Pyotr, Sergey.
Catalan: habitational name from a place so named in the district of La Garrotxa, Catalonia.
Basque: topographic name for someone who lived by a wood, from baso ‘wood, forest’.

nameMeanings.name: Osmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 19th-century revival of an Old English personal name composed of ōs ‘god’ + mund ‘protector’. The name was also in use among the Normans and was borne by an 11th-century saint who was appointed to the see of Salisbury by William the Conqueror. As a modern given name it may be in part a transferred use of the surname derived from this name.

en.lifeSummary: When Osmund Basset was born in 1105, in Colston Bassett, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Ralph Basset Justicar, was 31 and his mother, Alice de Buci, was 31. He had at least 3 sons with Basilia.

ko.lifeSummary: Osmund Basset(이)가 1105년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 콜스턴 배싯에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Ralph Basset Justicar(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Buci(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Basilia(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다.
